plan_type_rationale: Rule 6 fallthrough: focused CLI UX fix in a single file. No multi-layer impact, no architectural change.

phases.title: Audit, Fix, and Verify CLI Path UX
phases.description: Locate the coderef-pipeline.js CLI entry point, audit how --project-dir is parsed, implement the UX fix (error message + --help + optional positional fallback), rebuild if needed, test the fix, update CLI.md if --help changed, and commit.

tasks.description: Locate the coderef-pipeline CLI entry point: check src/cli/coderef-pipeline.js, dist/src/cli/coderef-pipeline.js, or bin/. Read it and identify the arg parser used (yargs/minimist/commander) and how --project-dir is currently declared. Confirm whether positional args are supported.
tasks.success_criteria: Exact source file path identified; arg parser library confirmed; current --project-dir declaration read; positional support status known

tasks.description: Run `coderef-pipeline --help` (or `node <entry> --help`) and capture current output. Confirm --project-dir is listed and what the current error behavior is when --project-dir is omitted.
tasks.success_criteria: Current --help output captured; silent-fail behavior on missing --project-dir confirmed

tasks.description: In the identified CLI source file: (a) add validation that errors with a clear actionable message when --project-dir is not provided: 'Error: --project-dir <path> is required. Example: coderef-pipeline scan --project-dir /path/to/project'; (b) update .option() description for --project-dir to mark it as required and explain its purpose; (c) if the arg parser supports positional args cleanly, add positional path as an alias for --project-dir.
tasks.success_criteria: Running `coderef-pipeline` with no --project-dir prints the error message. `--help` shows --project-dir prominently. Existing `--project-dir <path>` invocations still work.

tasks.description: If the source is TypeScript: run `npm run build` or `tsc` in the CODEREF-CORE root to compile. Then run a smoke test: `coderef-pipeline` (no args) → expect error message. `coderef-pipeline --project-dir C:/Users/willh/Desktop/CODEREF/CODEREF-CORE` → expect normal pipeline behavior (or at minimum no path-missing error).
tasks.success_criteria: Build succeeds. Missing-path error test: prints error message with --project-dir hint. With-path test: does not print path-missing error.

tasks.description: If --help output changed: update CODEREF-CORE/docs/CLI.md to reflect the new --project-dir flag description and any new positional arg syntax.
tasks.success_criteria: CLI.md reflects current --help output for coderef-pipeline. If --help unchanged, skip.

tasks.description: Stage and commit all changes: CLI source, docs/CLI.md (if changed). Message: fix(coderef-pipeline): add --project-dir required error + --help UX improvements
tasks.success_criteria: Clean git commit. No untracked changes in staged files.
